rulings.Case Title: DARYL BERKSON BRAND VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Omtion To Compel Further Responses To Demand For Production And Insepction Of Documetns And Things, Set One; Request For Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, April 11, 2012, Line 1, PLAINTIFF DARYL BRAND, THE ESTATE OF WILLIAM BRAND, DECEASED Motion To Compel Further Responses To Demand For Production And Insepction Of Documetns And Things, Set One; Request For Order- Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: IBRAHIM BASMA et al VS. ORIANA V. ZILL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Pltf'S Attendance At Deposition; Memo Of P & A; Req. For Reasonable Sanctions And Attorney Fees; Dec And Pos
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, April 11, 2012, Line 2, DEFENDANT ORIANA ZILL'S MOTION To Compel Pltf'S Attendance At Deposition;Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted, with monetary sanctions awarded against plaintiffs Ibrahim Basma, Abess Basma, and Hussein Basma in the amount of $ 1,386.30 (assuming no hearing and therefore reducing requested amount by $240 for two hours). The Motion is granted on the grounds set forth in the moving papers, and: (1) the Motion is not opposed. (2) Plaintiffs have not submitted any response or proposed response to the discovery propounded. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to katherinehuibonhoa@paulhastings.com referencing the case name and number in the subject line, with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/HK

rulings.Case Title: JOHNA PECOT et al VS. SAN FRANCISCO DEPUTY SHERIFF'S ASSOCIATION, A et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For Judgmnt On The Pleadings; Req For Judicial Notice
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, April 11, 2012, Line 3, DEFENDANTs DAVID WONG, MICHAEL ZEHNER, BRIAN SAVAGE, SHEDRICK MCDANIELS' Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings - Granted with leave to amend. Plaintiff has properly pled a cause of action under Cal. Corp. Code §§ 6333 and 6334 and the Court grants leave to amend to allow Plaintiff the opportunity to separately state a cause of action for concealment. The Court cannot look outside the face of the pleadings in ruling on a motion for judgment on the pleadings. The sixth cause of action is not res judicata of the U.S. District Court case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JJM)

rulings.Case Title: MARK HOOSHMAND VS. PHILIP MILLENBAH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Full And Complete Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set One And Request For Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, April 11, 2012, Line 3, PLAINTIFF MARK HOOSHMAND'S Motion To Compel Full And Complete Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set One And Request For Production Of Documents Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant Philip Millenbah states that he did not receive notice of this hearing until Friday April 6, 2012. The Court notes that plaintiff Mark Hooshmand has included a Proof of Service in his moving papers. The Court orders the hearing on this matter continued to May 8, 2012 in order to give Defendant additional time in which to respond; the Court does not, however, grant Defendant's request that Plaintiff produce documents, as that is not the subject of any motion. The Court further orders the parties to meet and confer further in an effort to resolve their dispute(s) and avoid the need for any hearing on this matter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to katherinehuibonhoa@paulhastings.com referencing the case name and number in the subject line, with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/HK

rulings.Case Title: AMY PITNER VS. ROBERT RASNUSSEN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Pltf'S Deposition And Request For Sanctions Against Pltf And Her Counsel Of Record
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, April 11, 2012, Line 4, DEFENDANTS RASMUSSEN, ROBERT AND AVIS BUDGET CAR RENTAL, LLC'S Motion To Compel Pltf'S Deposition And Request For Sanctions Against Pltf And Her Counsel Of Record Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Denied. The moving party failed to provide courtesy copies of the moving papers in compliance with San Francisco Local Rule 2.6B. Furthermore, there appears to be an appropriate basis upon which to postpone Plaintiff's deposition, based on Plaintiff's counsel's representation that they will seek to consolidate this action with another action and offer Plaintiff for a single deposition in the consolidated matter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to katherinehuibonhoa@paulhastings.com referencing the case name and number in the subject line, with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/HK

rulings.Case Title: WENDY DUJARDIN VS. RODAN AND FIELDS, LLC A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR LEAVE TO To File First Amended Complt
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, April 11, 2012, Line 8, PLAINTIFF WENDY DUJARDIN's MOTION FOR LEAVE To File First Amended Complaint - Motion for Leave to file First Amended Complaint is GRANTED. Request that First Amended Complaint be deemed filed and served is DENIED. Plaintiff is ordered to file and serve First Amended Complaint. Defendant may be relieved from any prejudice occasioned by the filing of the amended complaint by timely moving to continue trial and extending discovery and summary motion deadlines. The strong policy of liberally granting amendments and any prejudice to defendants eliminated by the trial continuance mandates the granting of the motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JJM)

rulings.Case Title: NETLINE CORPORATION, VS. LOOMIS GROUP INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Application And Hearing For Right To Attach Order; Order For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment; Application For Right To Attach Order Order For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, April 11, 2012, Line 19, PLAINTIFF NETLINE CORPORATION's Application And Hearing For Right To Attach Order; Order For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment - Application for Right to Attach Order and Writ of Attachment is GRANTED. Plaintiff Netline Corporation submitted sufficient admissible evidence to establish its prima facie case, including evidence that Loomis Group acknowledged the debt. Defendant Loomis Group did not object to this evidence and failed to support its claimed defenses with admissible evidence. Plaintiff's objections the Declaration of Jeffrey Loomis Declaration are sustained. Plaintiff's application and supporting evidence are sufficient to satisfy the "probable validity" standard. Plaintiff to post a $10,000 bond. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JJM)
